label: Forge Syndicate
description: A sprawling industrial labor union and manufacturing cartel that controls the ore refineries, foundries, and factories of Ironhold. Born from worker rebellions against corporate exploitation, the Syndicate now wields power through collective action, supply-chain monopoly, and ruthless protection of worker interests and collective profits. They negotiate with suppliers, set wage standards, and hold veto power over all major manufacturing contracts in the city. Their philosophy: labor creates value, and labor decides who profits. They are neither gentle nor forgiving with those who undercut wages or break solidarity.

role.label: Floor Worker (Union Member)
role.prerequisites: Sworn into the Syndicate via union initiation; committed to collective wage standards.
description: Guaranteed minimum wage (15 credits/day) and standard 8-hour shifts
Representation in wage disputes and workplace safety violations
Access to Syndicate mutual-aid fund in times of hardship or injury
Right to participate in strike votes and collective decisions
Discounts (15%) on tools, equipment, and goods sold through union suppliers

role.label: Shift Supervisor
role.prerequisites: Five years as floor worker, demonstrated leadership and Syndicate loyalty, elected by peers.
description: Earn 25 credits/day; oversee 2d6 workers on your shift
Authority to enforce Syndicate safety protocols and wage standards on your floor
Attend monthly Syndicate steward meetings; voice in local labor decisions
Requisition small batches of high-quality materials for personal projects at cost
Represent Syndicate interests in negotiations with factory management

role.label: Syndicate Steward
role.prerequisites: Shift Supervisor for three years, demonstrated oratory and political skill, elected to Syndicate council.
description: Earn 40 credits/day as steward; maintain voting power as worker
Council seat; direct input on Syndicate-wide wage negotiation, strike calls, and supply contracts
Authority to mediate labor disputes across multiple floors and factories
Access to Syndicate legal fund for defense against corporate retaliation
Can call 2d6 workers for solidarity actions or walkouts when needed
Potential to negotiate directly with factory owners on behalf of workers

role.label: Executive Coordinator
role.prerequisites: Steward for five years, led successful negotiations, unanimous council endorsement.
description: Senior leadership role; negotiate master contracts between Syndicate and major manufacturers
Control over supply agreements; can approve or veto raw material sources
Command authority: mobilize 3d6 solidarity picketers or 2d4 coordinated work stoppages across factories
Seat at highest Syndicate council; strategic decision-making power
Authority to enforce Syndicate discipline and expulsion for scab behavior
Direct leverage over wages, conditions, and profit-sharing across the city
